title: Mommy Nearest

jokes.text: It's round, it's rubber and you'll never use it.
jokes.setup: Will teases about what's in the bag he brought
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a condom, implying Carlton won't need it
jokes.explanation: Classic Will roast of Carlton with sexual innuendo. Well-structured misdirection with economy of language.

jokes.text: Carlton, can we please keep your hand puppet out of this?
jokes.setup: Following the condom joke
jokes.explanation: Follow-up innuendo joke. Decent structure but relies on crude humor without much sophistication.

jokes.text: Look, it's a California's driver's license. I think you're confused, Will. You need a license to drive a car, not steal one.
jokes.setup: Will shows off his new driver's license
jokes.punchline: Carlton implies Will steals cars instead of driving legally
jokes.explanation: Good character-based comedy playing on class/race stereotypes through Carlton's privileged perspective.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Carlton, I didn't understand you, I don't speak pygmy.
jokes.setup: Carlton makes a comeback at Will
jokes.explanation: Standard short joke. Functional but relies on basic physical mockery.

jokes.text: No. I hate family reunions. I always do all the work.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Phil is excited about family reunion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's practical frustration vs. Will's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Would you pick me up a gallon of Rocky Road and some sardines.
jokes.setup: Pregnant Vivian sends Phil to store
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy craving joke. Predictable but serviceable.

jokes.text: And don't eat it all on the way home like you did last time.
jokes.setup: Following the ice cream request
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke about Phil's weight issues. Well-integrated into his character.

jokes.text: I'm guessing, the guy with no rhythm.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Frank Jr. got his beautiful green eyes
jokes.punchline: Will implies it's from the white father
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical racial humor with perfect timing. Classic Will Smith delivery style.

jokes.text: You mean Carlton?
jokes.setup: Following Will's 'no rhythm' comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that builds on the previous joke and Carlton's character traits.

jokes.text: That's 'cause she be spending a lot of time in front of the microwave.
jokes.setup: Someone compliments Vivian's glow
jokes.punchline: Will attributes it to microwave radiation
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of pregnancy glow compliment with modern tech humor.

jokes.text: Well, you certainly have the pants for it.
jokes.setup: Phil jokes about getting pregnant next time
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback about Phil's size. Good economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, if I would have said that I would have gotten smacked in my head.
jokes.setup: After Helen's roast of Phil
jokes.punchline: Will points out the double standard
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on family dynamics and Will's position. Smart observational humor.

jokes.text: Now that's the first time a man has smiled at me since my divorce.
jokes.setup: Looking at baby Frank
jokes.punchline: Sad commentary on her post-divorce dating life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about divorce loneliness delivered through baby interaction. Well-crafted character moment.

jokes.text: But I don't like to badmouth him without the kid around. You know, it's like doing the work twice.
jokes.setup: Talking about hating her ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about divorce bitterness. Unexpected efficiency angle makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, like I really want some old lady's snot rag.
jokes.setup: Offered grandmother's silk handkerchief
jokes.explanation: Crude but in-character response. Economy works but relies on gross-out factor.

jokes.text: Maybe she got cable.
jokes.setup: Family speculating about Vy's 'big news'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of family drama. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Although, he's not a brother. I mean he is a brother... because he's my brother, but he's just not your kind of brother.
jokes.setup: Trying to set up Vy with her brother
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy about racial awkwardness. Poor economy but good character work.

jokes.text: Oh, brother.
jokes.setup: Following Janice's brother ramble
jokes.explanation: Perfect pun with maximum economy. Great comedic timing and wordplay.

jokes.text: No, I think I'm going to stick with the black folks on this one.
jokes.setup: Asked to help explain the brother situation
jokes.punchline: Will chooses racial solidarity over helping
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based racial humor. Will's smart deflection with perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: What kind of car he want to get me, Mom?
jokes.setup: After saying he's glad mom dumped Robert
jokes.punchline: Immediately interested when money is mentioned
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's conflicted feelings about money vs. principles.

jokes.text: Sir, when she's not looking, break one of her crystal figurines. It'll work wonders for your attitude.
jokes.setup: Phil complaining about being ordered around
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - subversive advice delivered with British propriety. Great character integration.

jokes.text: That does it, I'm getting me a white man.
jokes.setup: Watching Frank treat his wife well
jokes.explanation: Bold racial humor with great economy. Provocative but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: No, just to help around the house a little bit.
jokes.setup: After saying she's getting a white man
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's just for domestic help
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that softens the previous joke while maintaining the humor.

jokes.text: Now, come on, you know you all were thinking it.
jokes.setup: After the white man comments
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to acknowledge the thought
jokes.explanation: Meta moment that addresses audience reaction. Decent structure but not exceptional.

jokes.text: A horny husband.
jokes.setup: Helen asks what Eleanor has that she doesn't
jokes.explanation: Blunt sexual humor with great economy. Simple but effective punchline.

jokes.text: Hey, call me Will, man. What you say, you take me down to one of them dance clubs... you all be going to.
jokes.setup: Al trying to be cool with Will
jokes.explanation: Standard old man trying to be hip humor. Serviceable character comedy.

jokes.text: Never mind, boy, I thought you was hip. Get on out of my house.
jokes.setup: Will doesn't respond enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Al's quick mood change. Decent structure and timing.

jokes.text: It ain't nobody, Mama. Get your big old butt back in here then.
jokes.setup: Al flirting gets interrupted by his mother
jokes.explanation: Standard momma's boy humor. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Lord, who'd of thought he'd shoot up 12 inches... in his freshman year.
jokes.setup: Looking at Carlton's photo saying he's tall
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo humor. Functional but relies on cheap sexual implication.

jokes.text: Because I'm a 72-year-old man still living with his mother.
jokes.setup: Asked why he won't wear the leisure suit
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about his pathetic situation. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Can I have $10 to go to the movies?
jokes.setup: After declaring his independence
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for money like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic undercut of his independence speech. Excellent structure and character work.

jokes.text: Damn, got to use more dynamite next time.
jokes.setup: Asked if Robert's wife is dead yet
jokes.punchline: Implies she's trying to kill her
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor with great economy. Unexpected escalation makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I swear that man's part jackhammer.
jokes.setup: Saying Lou woke him up
jokes.explanation: Clever sexual metaphor with good economy. Vivid but slightly crude humor.

jokes.text: Well, I guess you old enough to remember.
jokes.setup: Comment about eating a lot at Last Supper
jokes.punchline: Implies the aunt is biblical-age old
jokes.explanation: Classic age insult with religious reference. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'm still young enough to kick your butt.
jokes.setup: Will's Last Supper joke
jokes.explanation: Good comeback showing feisty character. Standard but effective response.

jokes.text: Personally, I prefer eating. You don't have to wait an hour for seconds.
jokes.setup: Following up on eating vs sex
jokes.explanation: Clever sexual humor with good structure. Smart comparison that's memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Can we just for once have a conversation without my name being mentioned?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions 'What's Love Got to Do with It'
jokes.punchline: Tina Turner breaks fourth wall about her song being referenced
jokes.explanation: Decent meta joke but feels like celebrity cameo rather than organic humor

jokes.text: No, but I wish you would.
jokes.setup: Asked if she tells others how to raise kids
jokes.punchline: Admits she wishes she could get parenting advice
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about her parenting struggles. Nice character moment.

jokes.text: Apparently, I gave you the impression that I really wanted to know.
jokes.setup: Will trying to talk about his problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line - brutal honesty delivered with British politeness. Excellent character integration and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Okay, Vivian, I told him everything you wanted me to... now give me my piece of pie.
jokes.setup: After supportive conversation with Will
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was bribed with pie
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Phil's food motivation and undermining the sincere moment.

jokes.text: I love them, take me. Take me.
jokes.setup: Mention of Fifth Dimension performing
jokes.explanation: Standard Carlton enthusiasm joke. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Well, you smell like broccoli.
jokes.setup: Will tells him to shut up
jokes.explanation: Weak random insult. Poor structure and relies on absurdity without setup.

jokes.text: Well, you smell like broccoli.
jokes.setup: Will tells Carlton to shut up
jokes.punchline: Carlton responds with random vegetable insult
jokes.explanation: Random Carlton insult that's perfectly in character for his weird comebacks

jokes.text: Look what the wind did to my hat.
jokes.setup: Tense family moment
jokes.explanation: Standard excuse humor. Visual element helps but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: And whatever else of them holidays... it'd take to get that evil look out your face.
jokes.setup: Promising to visit for holidays
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will reading his mother's mood. Nice balance of humor and emotion.

jokes.text: Sir, when she's not looking, break one of her crystal fusiliers.
jokes.setup: Repeating earlier advice
jokes.explanation: Callback with malapropism. Decent but not as strong as the original joke.

jokes.text: They must got them in London.
jokes.setup: Phil asking what a fusilier is
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan ending to the malapropism sequence. Good character consistency.

tmdb_overview: Will tries to tell his mother that he doesn't want to return to Philadelphia with her after his high school graduation.
